prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response: In the shadow of rolling fog, the lighthouse stood sentinel on the treacherous coastline. The keeper, an aging, solitary figure, tended to the beacon with ritual precision, each sweep of light an echo through the mist. With the storm brewing outside, he felt the sudden urge to take an unaccustomed long walk down the spiraling staircase. Smoke spilling from his lips, he peered out through the briny air when an odd glimmer caught his eye. A tiny glow, like the flicker of an errant firefly, drifted over the waves. His heart racing, he followed its course, navigating treacherous cliffs. Upon the rocky shore, concealed by thick fog, lay an ancient, barnacle-encrusted bottle, emitting an ethereal light. Inside, the remnants of a map fluttered, its inks alive with luminescence. The keeper marveled, a sense of destiny twining through him. As the tempest howled closer, he hoisted the bottle high, knowing his quiet life was about to change with the promise of hidden treasures whispered by the mysterious sea.
